key_departures.impact: Bush was the lone defensive starter hitting free agency and was second on the team with 125 tackles in 2025. Cleveland reportedly passed on matching the Bears' offer. Immediate hole at the LB2 spot next to Carson Schwesinger.

Njoku publicly announced he would not return to Cleveland, ending a 9-year tenure. With Harold Fannin Jr. emerging as the new starter, the loss is partially mitigated, but depth and a veteran TE2 are now needed.
Teller publicly announced his departure, ending 7 seasons as Cleveland's starting RG. One of four Week 1 OL starters to hit free agency, compounding a massive IOL rebuild need.

Seven-time Pro Bowler and two-time First-Team All-Pro contemplating retirement for third consecutive offseason. His contract void date was pushed to the start of the league year (March 11). The Browns' signing of Zion Johnson at LG signals Bitonio is likely departing or retiring. If he retires, Cleveland faces $23.5M in dead cap that can be spread across two years.

Starting center tore his Achilles in Week 14. Contract voids for just $4.3M dead cap. Cleveland likely moving on, with Luke Wypler (2023 6th-round pick) as the internal candidate. Center market is thin and top-heavy — Linderbaum signed with Raiders.
Starting RT effectively replaced by the Tytus Howard trade. Conklin's departure was telegraphed by the Howard acquisition. Depth loss at OT.
Robinson started 12 of 13 games at LT after being acquired mid-season from Houston in 2025. His departure leaves a critical hole at LT, where Cleveland has no clear starter on the roster.
Thomas played all 17 games in 2025 (2.5 sacks, 7 QB hits). A productive rotational piece who signed with Atlanta on a 1-year, $3.1M deal. Modest loss in pass-rush depth behind Myles Garrett.

Emerson missed all of 2025 with a torn Achilles. Given he was injured in a contract year, the Browns are unlikely to re-sign him at starter money. His departure creates a CB depth question alongside Denzel Ward and Tyson Campbell.
Backup/swing guard who started several games down the stretch. Depth loss at IOL.
Veteran depth safety. Grant Delpit and tenderd Ronnie Hickman remain. Minimal impact.

contract.notes: 3-year extension ($63M per NFL.com/Rapoport) on top of existing 2026 contract year; Spotrac records the new extension as 2yr/$45M starting in 2027 per agent; OTC shows $36M guaranteed on previous Houston extension structure. Contract detail discrepancy between sources — NFL.com/Rapoport reports 3yr/$63M extension; ESPN/Schefter via agent reports 2yr/$45M starting 2027. Using NFL.com/Rapoport primary report; guaranteed TBD on new extension.

signings.analysis: Howard fills the RT vacancy immediately. A former 1st-round pick who started 93 consecutive games and allowed zero sacks in 2025 per PFF. However, he's 30 and primarily a right tackle — he does not address the glaring LT hole left by Cam Robinson's departure. With Howard locked in at RT and Dawand Jones returning from injury as a candidate, the LT position remains an urgent Tier 1 need. The 3yr extension keeps him through 2028+ but the OT need is only half-resolved.

Johnson was a top priority entering the tampering period and was signed quickly. The 3-year, $32.4M guaranteed deal signals a real investment — this is their guy at one guard spot. Johnson played 100% of Chargers snaps and has durability (missed only 2 games in 4-year career). However, his pass-block metrics were poor in 2025 (5th-lowest win rate among guards). He fills one of the two guard vacancies (Teller, Bitonio both gone/going), but center (Pocic departing) and a second guard remain unaddressed. The IOL rebuild is only partially complete — center is still a major hole.

contract.notes: Two-year deal worth up to $17M total per reporting ($8.5M/yr). Exact guarantee structure TBD — estimated at ~$9-10M based on 2-year mid-tier LB market.

signings.analysis: Cleveland moved quickly to replace Devin Bush (lost to Chicago on a 3yr/$30M deal) by signing Williams, who has a pre-existing relationship with new DC Mike Rutenberg from their time together with the Jets. Williams is a former First-Team All-Pro (2023) with four consecutive 100-tackle seasons under Rutenberg. However, he regressed in 2025 (83 tackles in 13 games) and has coverage concerns. At $8.5M/yr vs. Bush's $10M/yr, it's a moderate downgrade in current production. The 2-year structure limits long-term commitment. LB goes from escalated-need to partially addressed — the pairing with DROY Carson Schwesinger gives Cleveland a potentially solid duo, but Williams must prove his 2025 regression was a scheme issue.

contract.notes: Second-round tender at the estimated 2026 tender value of ~$5.767M per Spotrac.

signings.analysis: Hickman was a full-time starter in 2025 (17 starts, 103 tackles, 2 INTs, 7 PDs). The second-round tender retains him for 2026 and protects the Browns' right of first refusal. This is a roster-maintenance move, not addressing any unmet need — safety was not a pressing need with Hickman and Grant Delpit under contract.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Howard trade (3yr/$63M extension) locks in the RT spot with a proven starter. However, LT remains completely vacant — Cam Robinson is gone, Dawand Jones is recovering from his third consecutive season-ending injury, and the Browns have no clear LT starter. OT is still a critical early-round draft priority, just shifted from 'both tackles' to 'LT specifically.' Tier drops from 1 to 2 only because RT is now solved.

Zion Johnson (3yr/$49.5M, $32.4M gtd) fills one guard spot with a real starter-level investment. But center (Pocic gone, Wypler is developmental) and the second guard position (Bitonio all-but-confirmed departing/retiring) remain open. IOL remains a high-priority draft need — center in particular is unaddressed in free agency after Linderbaum went to the Raiders. Reduces from Tier 1 to Tier 2 because one starter is in place.

Quincy Williams (2yr/$17M) replaces Devin Bush at a slight value discount. Williams is a known starter with Rutenberg familiarity, and Schwesinger (2025 DROY) anchors the position. LB drops one tier — it's no longer a critical need, but the regression risk on Williams and the 2-year bridge structure means a developmental LB in the mid-rounds is still smart.

Njoku's confirmed departure creates a TE2 need. Harold Fannin Jr. is the clear starter and franchise building block after a strong 2025 rookie season, so TE1 is not a need. But the Browns need a TE2/pass-catching complement for Todd Monken's multi-TE sets. This is now an active need the team should address in rounds 3–5 or via a veteran free agent signing.

Martin Emerson Jr. departing (Achilles year) removes a starter-level CB. Cleveland has Denzel Ward and Tyson Campbell as starters, but depth is thin and Ward is aging. Emerson's injury complicates the depth chart. CB needs to be addressed in the middle rounds of the draft to ensure viable depth.

No WR has been signed. Cleveland's receiver room produced the fewest receiving yards in the NFL in 2025 (1,467 yards). Jerry Jeudy is the primary target, but there is no credible WR2 or vertical threat. Alec Pierce went to Indy at a record price; other top receivers are off the board or out of Cleveland's budget. With a new OC (Monken) who requires weapons, WR is now the team's most urgent remaining offensive need. Should be addressed early in the draft (round 1 or 2) and possibly via free agency.

No QB move has been made in free agency. Deshaun Watson (restructured for $36M cap savings) remains on the roster in a backup/bridge role. The team holds the No. 2 overall pick and is strongly linked to Shedeur Sanders. QB remains a first-round certainty. Free agency QB options (Kyler Murray, Geno Smith, Aaron Rodgers) are being monitored but no deal made.

Myles Garrett's 4yr/$160M extension (signed March 2025) locks in the best EDGE in football through 2030. Cameron Thomas (rotational, 2.5 sacks) left for Atlanta, creating a depth hole. Alex Wright and Isaiah McGuire remain. EDGE is not a premium need with Garrett secured, but a depth/developmental EDGE rusher in rounds 3–5 is still prudent.

Ethan Pocic (Achilles) not returning, Tyler Linderbaum signed with Raiders, and center market is thin. Luke Wypler (2023 6th-round pick) is the only internal option and is unproven as a starter. Center is arguably the single most urgent unaddressed need remaining in free agency and should be a primary draft target.

key_additions.headline: Former 1st-round pick and 2025 zero-sack-allowed tackle locks down the RT spot, anchoring Cleveland's OL rebuild
First-round pick and ironman guard fills one critical IOL vacancy with real long-term investment
Former All-Pro reunites with DC Rutenberg to replace Bush and pair alongside DROY Schwesinger
Browns retain full-time starting safety on second-round tender after breakout 2025 season

key_losses.headline: Resurgent LB (125 tackles, 3 INTs) cashes in with Chicago after Cleveland declined to match
9-year Browns TE publicly announced departure, leaving Harold Fannin Jr. as the clear TE1
Veteran starting RG posted social media farewell, ending 7-year run as a Cleveland anchor
7x Pro Bowl LG mulling retirement for third straight year; Zion Johnson signing signals end of the Bitonio era
Starting LT from 2025 departs, leaving Cleveland without a clear left tackle starter heading into the draft
Starting center recovering from torn Achilles likely departs, opening a massive void at the position
Starting CB missed all of 2025 with Achilles; Browns unlikely to re-sign at starter money
Productive rotational edge (2.5 sacks, 7 QB hits) departs for Atlanta on a modest 1-year deal

notes: Note on Tytus Howard contract: There is a discrepancy between sources. NFL.com/Rapoport report a 3yr/$63M extension putting Howard under contract for 4 total years. ESPN/Schefter via Howard's agent reports a 2yr/$45M extension starting in 2027 (with 2026 already under contract). OTC shows the prior HOU extension structure. This JSON uses the NFL.com figure as primary. Guarantee details on the new CLE extension have not been fully published. Joel Bitonio's status remains officially unresolved as of March 10 — his contract void date is March 11 (start of league year). His departure/retirement is treated here as a confirmed departure given strong contextual signals (Zion Johnson signed at LG, Browns HC Monken confirmed Bitonio had not committed to return). If Bitonio returns, IOL need tier should be revised downward.
